title: Kewa trachysperma
taxonomy: Kewa trachysperma is classified as a species within the family Sapindaceae, which includes other trees such as maples and lychees. The Sapindaceae family is part of the larger order Sapindales, which comprises flowering plants like cashews and pistachios. Kewa trachysperma is native to parts of Africa and is known for its unique characteristics and uses. The classification of Kewa trachysperma reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Sapindaceae family. Kewa trachysperma's classification provides valuable insights for botanists and researchers studying plant diversity and evolution.
